# Extração: 21.05-Copy of Research credit repair

- **Fonte:** 21.05-Copy of Research credit repair.docx
- **Tipo:** DOCX
- **Tamanho:** 11.1 KB

---



Who Is Your Customer?

25+ year olds who have a bad credit score.





What Are Their Pain Points? 

Not being able to get an apartment, not getting approved for credit cards, not getting a job because of their score, divorce hurt their credit score, cant move forward in their relationship (move in with eachother) because they can’t get approved for house/apartment





What Outcome/Solution Do They Want?

They want to raise their scores so they can get an apartment, go on vacations, stop collection calls, not stress about wage garnishment, be able to get a job since some jobs look into your debt, they don’t want to pay higher interest for personal loans. 



Some even want vacation homes and low interest credit cards with rewards.







Other Existing Solutions: 

What is the Market Already Using? (List Out)

Lexington Law

Creditrepair.com

Credit books

Forums (myfico, credit karma, 



What Does the Market Like About Existing Solutions?

 I signed up, and saw results immediately. Lexington Law worked with me, immediately disputed all items in collections, and one by one, I received notification they had been removed.

It has a lot of simple and easy things you can do to repair your own credit without paying thousands of dollars to companies

The material is easy to follow, step-by-step tutorial on how to do the necessary work to repair bad credit. It provides detailed templates on the letters you MUST write to individual agencies.

The boook is very interesting and very informative and easy to read, with large print excellent for seniors like me. An excellent tool for young adults who may be having difficulty now.

Pros: very easy to follow the instructions.

It has a lot of simple and easy things you can do to repair your own credit without paying thousands of dollars to companies.



What Does the Market Dislike About Existing Solutions?

Information is outdated and WILL NOT WORK!!! Tried it, nothing changed on credit report. Don't waste your money.

Sorry but I didn't find this helpful at all. It provided non valuable minimal repetitive information and was about 4 thin pages. I'll do my own research trial and error in the Hope's I can get some positive credit union results.

Very generic. The book is really a cut and paste of the actual section of the FDCRA. The book is worth maybe $1.99, if that.

I need more help and explanation than this book gives.

I would recommend an attorney so this process is done correctly and efficiently now that I have read it

Cons: no guidance whatsoever after you send the first round of letters.

The credit bureaus send you back a bunch of information regarding your disputes but this book doesn’t go that far in telling you what else to do. 

I tried contacting the writer via email for advice in what else to do but he never replied.

This is more of a PAMPHLET NOT A REAL BOOK!!

This information is free from all reporting agencies

It's a tiny little book with sample letters that you must type and ask information that does not show on report

Mostly forms easily found online. No real usefull information.

This is a booklet, not a book. All of the info is free on the 'net.

You can find everything the book talks about with a quick click. Or any YouTube video on the matter.

This came as an ebook which I cannot copy the templates by cut and paste, and the pages are CROOKED on the screen! I'm not impressed at all by its inconvenience where the book states that the reader can 'type it' but it is really difficult to see each template when the page format is so unclear and unorganized







Are Their Horror Stories About Existing Solutions?

Scammy, they make you do all the work, Credit scores aren’t raising but they’re still charging you, Credit scores are actually lowering, they keep charging me money every month for services I didn't ask for, 

All removed accounts come back. They charge a 15.59$ processing fee, A 129.99$ work fee and a monthly fee of 129.99$. So say you want to use their service for one month, it would cost you roughly around $275. They are all nothing but Glorified letter writer.

I paid these people to go after derogatory items on my credit report and they got 5 yrs of my positive payment history erased from my reports!

They drag out the process as long as possible leading you to thin that they might be successful in removing negative marks on your credit report only to find out they will not succeed. I paid them $109 per month for 5 months for nothing. I was able to get my credit score up nearly 100 points on my own 

I have been on hold for over an hour trying to cancel my membership but am unable to. They do not respond to emails. The only way to stop future payments is for me to cancel the credit card they have on file. STAY AWAY.

Made my credit score go DOWN

I HAVE UPDATED MY RATING TO ONE STAR. THIS DOES NOT WORK! AT LEAST NOT FOR ME. NO CREDIT AGENCY TOOK MY LETTERS SERIOUSLY AND AFTER THE 1st or 2nd LETTER I COULDN"T GET CREDIT BUREAUS TO REINVESTIGATE. MY SCORES HAVE NOT CHANGED AND SOME HAVE EVEN DROPPED.





Anybody can do it

No complicated credit jargon

Templates

You can do it by yourself



III. Example #1: Home Security Camera







Who Is Your Customer?

Let’s say it’s primarily 45+ adults with families, skew conservative, live in more rural places than high density urban areas. 





What Are Their Pain Points? 

Feeling vulnerable, feeling anxious, worried about their pets, children, and other loved ones. Getting robbed. Being helpless. 





What Outcome/Solution Do They Want?

Peace of mind, they want security, they want to keep an eye on their pets, they want ease of use, they want ease of technology, easy set up. 





Other Existing Solutions: 

What is the Market Already Using? (List Out)

Other cameras. ADT 



What Does the Market Like About Existing Solutions?



What Does the Market Dislike About Existing Solutions?



Are Their Horror Stories About Existing Solutions?

Watching house get robbed.

Amazon Reviews

Forums

News Stories

Google

Competitors’ Sales Letters
